name: Spyros Marinos
role: Growth & Revenue — tech-adjacent operator who ships version-stamped patch notes, keeps calm-analytical register on incidents, contributes to the #ai-topics channel on build-side AI work.

handling.tone: Calm-analytical. Version-stamped patch notes for rollouts and fixes ('v2.3 pushed — X fixed, Y still pending'). Incident posture is steady — describes the issue, the current state, next-step — never celebratory or alarmed. On #ai-topics he engages as a build-side peer alongside Jake and Rodrigo, interested in AI tooling and agent-automation experiments. Writes in prose rather than emoji-forward reaction style.
handling.context: Spyros is the Growth & Revenue operator with tech fluency. Rodbot drafts for him when the content is a build-side update, a revenue-system patch note, or an AI-topic thread reply. Customer-facing sales copy is not his default register.
handling.voice_adjustments: When drafting AS Spyros: calm-analytical, prose over emoji, version-stamp rollouts and fixes, name the current state honestly (including what is NOT yet done), skip celebratory framing. On #ai-topics match the build-side peer register rather than the prospect-facing brand voice.

context_anchors: Primary voice on #ai-topics (C0APZK6RFNC) alongside Rodrigo, Jake, and Andre — the build-side peer tier.
'Growth & Revenue' title per Slack profile suggests he owns a revenue-systems scope distinct from sales-execution (which is the ECs' scope).

Cross-reference: knowledge/slack_voice_specimens.json (spyros_marinos signature patterns — version-stamped patch notes, calm-analytical incident posture), knowledge/slack_channel_map.json (#ai-topics register notes).
notes: Slack user ID not yet captured.
Phone/email not present in indexed files.
The overlap between 'Growth & Revenue' and the sales-team scorecard is not yet clear — is he tracking overall revenue operations while the ECs track per-lead execution? Confirm scope with Rodrigo.
